messages.content:
The Eiffel Tower is a world-famous landmark located in Paris, France. It is an iron lattice tower named after its designer, Gustave Eiffel, whose engineering company was responsible for its construction. Standing at a height of **330 meters (1,083 feet)**, it is one of the tallest structures in France and an iconic symbol of French culture, architecture, and ingenuity.

The tower was built as the centerpiece of the 1889 **Exposition Universelle** (World's Fair) held to celebrate the 100th anniversary of the French Revolution. Originally criticized by some artists and intellectuals, the Eiffel Tower has since become one of the most recognizable structures in the world and is a major tourist attraction, drawing millions of visitors annually.

The tower has three levels accessible to the public. Visitors can take elevators or climb stairs to enjoy panoramic views of Paris from the observation decks. It is especially stunning when illuminated at night, thanks to thousands of sparkling lights.

Fun fact: The Eiffel Tower was once the world's tallest man-made structure until the Chrysler Building in New York City surpassed it in 1930.
